data.title: DWF Labs partners: Holders of USD1 can participate in Falcon Stable internal testing and Farm activities first
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: DWF Labs partners published a post saying that users who hold USD1 stablecoin issued by World Liberty Financial in their on-chain wallets can get priority access to Falcon Stable's closed beta test and participate in the Farm event first. Users need to forward the relevant tweets and leave the wallet address holding USD1 in the comment area.

data.title: JasmyCoin soars nearly 20%: what’s driving the surge?
data.description:
JasmyCoin jumps nearly 20% to $0. 0165 on April 12 amid tariff pause optimism. Technical breakout from a descending triangle suggests potential gains to $0. 058
source: https://coinjournal.net/news/jasmycoin-soars-nearly-20-whats-driving-the-surge/

data.title: CZ responds to WSJ report: Someone bribed reporters to discredit me, government witnesses will not go to jail
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Binance founder Changpeng Zhao (CZ) responded to the Wall Street Journal report, saying "WSJ is really trying hard to discredit me" and "they seem to have forgotten who is in jail and who is not." He pointed out that people who become government witnesses will not be sentenced, but will be protected instead. CZ also hinted that someone paid WSJ employees to publish negative news against him.

data.title: PAXG Trading Volume Surpasses $110 Million in 24 Hours
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, PAX Gold (PAXG) has seen its 24-hour trading volume exceed $110 million, reaching $112,492,427. The current price of PAXG is $3,269.70, marking a 0.8% increase over the past 24 hours. The total market capitalization of PAXG has risen to $736 million.

PAX Gold is a digital currency issued by Paxos, backed by gold. Each PAXG token is equivalent to one ounce of gold, and holding PAXG tokens represents ownership of physical gold held by Paxos Trust Company.

data.title:
I wonder how quick people's "muscle memory" is from these kind of messages.

We've seen this before after all.

Central banks putting out these messages generally means liquidity is coming sooner or later.

$BTC loves itself some fresh liquidity.
